title: Returning joining namespace error should not be fatal
body:
I got my database state in a bad way by killing a hanging container.

It did not setup the network namespace correctly

listing/remove bad containers becomes impossible.

podman run alpine/nginx
^c
got me in this state.

I got into a state in the database where
podman ps -a
was returning errors and I could not get out of it,  Makeing joining the network
namespace a non fatal error fixes the issue.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: If we're going to do this, we should mark the containers in question invalid (c.valid = false) before returning them, to make sure that we don't try and start a container with a bad netns. We'll need to do some work to runtime.RemoveContainer to allow removing invalid containers, but if we allow the containers to be valid and usable we could put the state in an even worse place.

@rhatdan as of right now, this will wedge containers in a state where we can't remove them - can you patch https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/blob/master/libpod/runtime_ctr.go#L167-L171 to check if the container exists with `r.state.HasContainer` and only return an error if it doesn't?
@mheon Your fix put the tests into the other test an we ended up in infinite loop.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably c1ef115) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@mheon Do you still think this will cause issues?
@rhatdan Let me take a look this afternoon... I'm really curious as to why my changes created infinite loops
@mheon Did you get a chance to look at this?

Alright. I thought I had an alternative way that would allow continued use of the container without removal, but boltdb read/write protection put the brakes on that. Tests seem to be good, and this is clearly a problem. LGTM
@rh-atomic-bot r+

:pushpin: Commit ecbca48 has been approved by `mheon`
